# omada_wireless_network (Resource)

Manages a wireless network (SSID) on the Omada Controller.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "omada_wireless_network" "example" {
  name          = "HomeWiFi"
  wlan_group_id = omada_wlan_group.default.id
  band          = 3 # Both 2.4GHz and 5GHz
  security      = 3 # WPA2/WPA3-Personal
  passphrase    = var.wifi_password
  broadcast     = true
  vlan_id       = 50
  enable_11r    = true # Fast BSS Transition (802.11r)
  pmf_mode      = 2    # Optional PMF
}

variable "wifi_password" {
  type      = string
  sensitive = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `name` (String) The SSID name (broadcast name).

### Optional

- `band` (Number) Radio band bitmask: 1=2.4GHz, 2=5GHz, 3=both.
- `broadcast` (Boolean) Whether the SSID is broadcast (visible). Set false for hidden networks.
- `enable_11r` (Boolean) Enable 802.11r Fast Roaming.
- `passphrase` (String, Sensitive) The Wi-Fi password (WPA pre-shared key). Required when security > 0.
- `pmf_mode` (Number) Protected Management Frames mode: 1=disabled, 2=optional, 3=required.
- `security` (Number) Security mode: 0=Open, 3=WPA2/WPA3. Note: do NOT use 2 (WPA2-only fails on v6).
- `vlan_id` (Number) Custom VLAN ID to assign to this SSID. If not set, uses the default VLAN.
- `wlan_group_id` (String) The WLAN group ID. If not set, the default WLAN group is used.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the SSID.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Import by wlan_group_id/ssid_id
terraform import omada_wireless_network.example 696a40fd49039e1d13a9c412/696a419049039e1d13a9c4d3
```
